description: The Dutch Ministry of Economic Affairs and Climate Policy (Ministerie van Economische Zaken en Klimaat, EZK) is the central-government ministry responsible for entrepreneurship, innovation, competition, the digital economy, energy and regional economic policy in the Netherlands.

entity.officialNameNl: Ministerie van Economische Zaken en Klimaat
entity.officialNameEn: Ministry of Economic Affairs and Climate Policy
entity.parent: Rijksoverheid — central government of the Kingdom of the Netherlands
entity.establishedLineage: Origins 1905 (Ministry of Agriculture, Industry & Commerce); renamed Ministry of Economic Affairs 1946; climate portfolio added 2017 → EZK; split into EZ + Klimaat en Groene Groei (KGG) under the Schoof cabinet (Jul 2024); re-merged as EZK under the Jetten cabinet (Feb 2026).

portfolio: Digital economy, telecom and digital sovereignty
State aid and foreign direct-investment screening
executiveAgencies: RVO — Rijksdienst voor Ondernemend Nederland (Netherlands Enterprise Agency)

leadership._note: Officeholders change with cabinets. Agent-researched against rijksoverheid.nl; treat as point-in-time and verify before external use — NOT a stable brand fact.
leadership.minister: Minister van Economische Zaken en Klimaat (Jetten cabinet, from 2026-02-23)

firmographics.legalName: Ministerie van Economische Zaken en Klimaat
firmographics._note: Brandfetch (rijksoverheid.nl, q=0.54, unclaimed) returned the 'Rijksoverheid' umbrella with role-confused colors and was NOT used for this ministry's identity — see charter.provenance.json.

_provenance.createdBy: brand-intelligence validated refresh (rijksoverheid.nl authoritative + Brandfetch trust-gated)
